title: skopeo-login doesn't appear to work on macOS Catalina.
body:
I get the following error message when trying to do a skopeo login:

```
$ brew install skopeo
...
Warning: skopeo 1.0.0 is already installed and up-to-date
$ skopeo --version
skopeo version 1.0.0
$ skopeo login myregistry.com -u myusername -p xxxx
FATA[0002] error creating directory "/run/containers/501": mkdir /run: read-only file system
```

I tried with sudo too and got the same error. The equivalent docker-login command works find though, which means that my registry url and login credentials are correct.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

I’m afraid the current default does not account for macOS.

Right now you can use `skopeo login --authfile ~/.docker/config.json …`, or set $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR to something (preferably matching https://specifications.freedesktop.org/basedir-spec/basedir-spec-latest.html ).

Cc: @rhatdan for another thing to design. It’s very tempting to just point the macOS default path to ~/.docker/config.json , if we are not benefiting from moving it to a directory with substantially different properties.

I can’t find any similarly-behaving directory in https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/FileManagement/Conceptual/FileSystemProgrammingGuide/Introduction/Introduction.html .

Alternatively, if we are considering system keyrings on Linux, it might make sense to ship a configuration that points at https://github.com/docker/docker-credential-helpers/tree/master/osxkeychain (disclaimer: I have never tried this).

Cc:ing recent contributors to the Brew formula for their opinions on such a default (or help if there is a different good solution we are missing): @chenrui333 @glensc @EricFromCanada

It might be better to create `~/. containers` folder, many MacOS software version managers are doing that (`rbenv`, `nvm`, `gvm`, `pyenv` and so on).

But we also ship `amazon-ecr-credential-helper` in homebrew side, so pointing to  `~/.docker/config.json` (`skopeo login --authfile ~/.docker/config.json …` also works for me (as user).

@QiWang19 PTAL
I think reading ~/.containers/config.json and ~/.docker/config.json Would be fine for MAC and Windows
@Sher-Chowdhury Is the "auth" value in base64 format? The auth file format should look like this https://github.com/containers/image/blob/master/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md
